items.name: Uruky
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Uruky (EU-based Kagi alternative) now has Image Search and URL Rewrites
items.summary: EU-based search engine with image search, URL rewrites, and API access.
items.why_included: Comments liked the privacy-first EU positioning and the expanding provider mix.
items.evidence: HN discussion highlighted image search, URL rewrites, a 2h free trial, and integrations like Mojeek, Marginalia, EUSP, Linkup, and Serper.

items.name: Anthropic vulnerability discovery framework
items.source_story_title: Anthropic's open-source framework for AI-powered vulnerability discovery
items.summary: Open-source harness for AI-assisted vulnerability discovery.
items.why_included: Security-focused LLM tooling drew the clearest practical praise in the thread.
items.evidence: Commenters framed security as a strong LLM use case and suggested adapting the harness to each team’s workflow instead of using it unchanged.

items.name: KVarN
items.source_story_title: KVarN: Native vLLM backend for KV-cache quantization by Huawei
items.summary: Native vLLM backend for KV-cache quantization.
items.why_included: Thread consensus was that it meaningfully improves speed, even if quality is not better than FP16.
items.evidence: Commenters debated the benchmark claims, concluded it is faster than FP16 rather than higher quality, and said a vLLM PR should be feasible.

items.name: Vite
items.summary: Frontend tooling stack that commenters treated as the best part of the VoidZero portfolio.
items.why_included: The acquisition thread turned into a praise session for Vite’s developer experience and ecosystem.
items.evidence: Comments said Vite made webpack-era tooling pain disappear and repeatedly referenced the broader stack—Vitest, Rolldown, Oxc, and Vite+.

items.name: Qwen 3.5 9B
items.source_story_title: Gemma 4 12B: A unified, encoder-free multimodal model
items.summary: Compact local model commenters recommended for laptops with limited VRAM.
items.why_included: A commenter singled it out as the best fit for 16GB laptops.
items.evidence: One reply called Qwen 3.5 9B the undisputed champ for 16GB laptops and compared it favorably against other small models.

items.name: gpt-oss-20b
items.source_story_title: Gemma 4 12B: A unified, encoder-free multimodal model
items.summary: Open-weight OpenAI model aimed at low-latency local or specialized use.
items.why_included: It showed up in the thread as one of the compact model options people are actually comparing.
items.evidence: Commenters grouped gpt-oss-20b with Qwen 3.5 9B and other small models as a local-inference candidate.
